key_insight: Extending atomic cluster expansion (ACE) training data can bring near-DFT accuracy for screw-dislocation glide in bcc refractory metals, but transferability remains highly element- and database-dependent.
engineering_relevance: More reliable dislocation-core and Peierls-barrier predictions can improve constitutive models for strength, temperature sensitivity, and plasticity in refractory metals used in extreme environments.
method: Built ACE interatomic potentials for V, Nb, Ta, Mo, and W by extending an existing DFT database.
Evaluated screw-dislocation properties, including Peierls barriers, glide planes, and kink-pair activation enthalpies.
Compared model predictions against DFT and simple validation criteria such as single-humped barriers.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports near-DFT agreement for Mo and W, substantial improvement for V, Nb, and Ta, and reliable kink-pair enthalpies for Nb, Mo, and W.
watch_item: A single-humped Peierls barrier is not sufficient to validate dislocation-glide fidelity, so database composition and element-specific energetics must be checked carefully.
paper_title: Atomic Cluster Expansion Potentials for Screw Dislocations in BCC Refractory Metals
